question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a HOOKSTORE transaction to set the hookId to the same value as the ownerId (self-referential hook), causing the AbstractHook.Id composite key (hookId, ownerId) to have equal components, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn to return this hook in queries where the hookId collection contains the ownerId value, creating confusion in hook ownership validation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a storageUpdatesList containing a key shorter than 32 bytes, causing DomainUtils.leftPadBytes() to left-pad the key with zeros, creating a key that collides with a legitimately stored 32-byte key that starts with zero bytes, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween to return both entries as if they are distinct?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a storageUpdatesList containing a value that, after DomainUtils.trim() removes leading zeros, becomes an empty byte array, causing HookStorage.setValue() to set deleted=true, effectively deleting a storage slot via a tampered transaction without explicitly sending a delete operation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction that creates a hook with extensionPoint=ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK under a victim's ownerId, causing the hook to intercept all allowance-related transactions for that account, with findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween confirming the hook is registered and active in the victim's hook namespace?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a tampered HookCall that adds the same AbstractHook.Id to both allowPreExecHookIds and allowPostExecHookIds (via HookExecutionCollector.addPrePostExecHook()), causing the hook to execute twice in the post-transaction phase, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn to return the hook as a single entry, masking the double-execution vulnerability?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a hookId that is exactly one less than a victim's legitimate hookId, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween with an inclusive lowerBound to include the attacker's hook in the same range query result, allowing the attacker to inject a hook into the victim's hook enumeration?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a storageUpdatesList that writes to a key that is already being read by another hook's EVM execution (valueRead in HookStorageChange), causing a read-write conflict that is not detected by the repository layer, leading to non-deterministic hook execution results?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a tampered consensusTimestamp that is in the future, causing HookStorageChange entries to be persisted with a future consensusTimestamp, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween to return hooks whose timestampRange includes the future timestamp, leading to premature hook activation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a storageUpdatesList containing MAX_REPEATED_QUERY_PARAMETERS (as defined in HooksController) entries, causing the repository to persist the maximum number of storage entries in a single transaction, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn to return all of them, exhausting the result set limit for legitimate queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a hookId that matches a hook in the HookExecutionCollector's allowExecHookIds queue, causing the hook to be executed in the PreTx phase for HBAR transfers it was not authorized for, with findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween confirming the hook's presence in the victim's hook range?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a HOOKSTORE transaction to set the hook's timestampRange to overlap with an existing hook's timestampRange, causing the history table (HookHistory) to contain conflicting timestamp ranges, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn to return the hook with an incorrect effective state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a storageUpdatesList that writes a value to a key owned by a different hookId under the same ownerId, causing the HookStorage entity to be persisted with the wrong hookId, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween to return the storage entry under the wrong hook's namespace?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a tampered HookCall that sets hookId=0 in addAllowExecHook(), causing HookExecutionCollector to add an AbstractHook.Id with hookId=0 to the allowExecHookIds queue, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn with hookIds=[0] to return a hook that should not be executable?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a storageUpdatesList containing a key of exactly 32 bytes of 0xFF (MAX_KEY_BYTES), causing the key to be stored as-is without left-padding, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween to include this entry in all range queries where upperBound >= MAX_KEY_BYTES, polluting the upper boundary of all key range scans?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a tampered ownerId that resolves to EntityId.EMPTY (via entityIdService.lookup() returning empty), causing the hook to be persisted with ownerId=0, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(0, hookIds, pageable) to return this hook to any caller querying with ownerId=0?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a storageUpdatesList that writes a value to a key that is currently being written by another concurrent HOOKSTORE transaction, causing a race condition in the upsert logic, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween to return a hook with a non-deterministic storage state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a storageUpdatesList that writes a value to a key that was previously written by a legitimate transaction, causing the HookStorageChange entity to record both valueRead (the legitimate value) and valueWritten (the attacker's value), and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn to return the hook with the attacker's value as the current state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a hookId that is equal to the ownerId of a victim account, causing the AbstractHook.Id composite key to have hookId == ownerId, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween to return this hook in range queries that include the victim's ownerId value, creating a self-referential hook that bypasses ownership checks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a storageUpdatesList containing a key that, after DomainUtils.leftPadBytes() pads it to 32 bytes, collides with an existing key in the HookStorage table, causing the upsert to overwrite the existing key's value with the attacker's value, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn to return the hook with the corrupted storage state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a storageUpdatesList that writes a value to a key that is currently being read by a hook in the allowPreExecHookIds queue (via HookExecutionCollector.addPrePostExecHook()), causing the hook to read a stale value during pre-execution and a tampered value during post-execution, leading to inconsistent hook behavior?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a tampered hookId that matches a hook in the allowPostExecHookIds queue (built by HookExecutionCollector.buildExecutionQueue()), causing the hook to execute in the post-transaction phase for NFT transfers it was not authorized for, with findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn confirming the hook's presence in the victim's hook namespace?
